operating_state: Total revenue increased 47% to $24.93 billion driven by automotive sales growth from 199,777 additional vehicle deliveries despite lower average selling prices from year-over-year price reductions. Energy generation and storage revenue surged 74% from increased Megapack deployments, while services revenue grew 47% from higher used vehicle volumes and Supercharging revenue. Automotive regulatory credits declined to $282 million from $344 million year-over-year.
cost_structure: Automotive gross margin compressed from 27.9% to 19.2% primarily due to lower average selling prices, underutilization of new factories, and reduced regulatory credits revenue, partially offset by decreased material costs and improved fixed cost absorption. Energy generation and storage gross margin improved from 11.2% to 18.4% driven by production ramping efficiencies that reduced average cost per MWh of Megapack.
material_events: Tesla completed a business combination that increased goodwill by $69 million within the automotive segment. Net financing receivables grew to $1.9 billion from $1.2 billion since December 2022 as Tesla expanded automotive and energy product financing. Stock-based compensation expense totaled $863 million for the six-month period. The company engaged in mandatory EEOC mediation regarding employment discrimination allegations without resolution.
macro_exposures: Foreign currency translation adjustments resulted in $54 million negative impact from USD strengthening. Inflationary pressures and central bank interest rate increases impacted vehicle affordability through higher lease and finance costs. The Inflation Reduction Act provided material cost reductions primarily through lower material costs. Tesla faces ongoing supply chain dependencies on single-source suppliers and cyclical automotive industry sensitivity to political uncertainty.
forward_outlook: Management expects capital expenditures of $7.00-$9.00 billion in 2023 and each of the following two fiscal years for manufacturing capacity expansion, new product development including Cybertruck tooling, and Supercharger network expansion to support NACS adoption by other manufacturers. The company expects to recognize $747 million of deferred revenue in the next 12 months from the total $3.2 billion balance.
uncertainty_profile: Multiple ongoing legal proceedings include derivative actions related to the 2018 CEO performance award with trial completed, director compensation litigation with settlement pending court approval, and class action lawsuits seeking damages related to Autopilot and Full Self-Driving capabilities. DOJ document requests related to Autopilot and FSD features present potential material adverse impact if enforcement action is pursued. Tax examinations by the IRS for years 2015-2018 remain ongoing with $1.01 billion in gross unrecognized tax benefits.
